title: Evening Times (Glasgow) — September 15, 2017 (Page M28)
author: Evening Times
card_summary: A clipped page from the Evening Times (Glasgow, Strathclyde, Scotland), dated Friday, September 15, 2017, page M28. Likely contains an article referencing Philip K. Dick, possibly in connection with adaptations or cultural reception of his work in the United Kingdom.
page_summary:
A scanned newspaper page from the Evening Times (Glasgow, Strathclyde, Scotland), dated Friday, September 15, 2017, page M28. The clipping was sourced from Newspapers.com and downloaded on April 15, 2025. The PDF text layer contains only source metadata with no recoverable article text.

This Scottish newspaper clipping is notable as a document of Dick's international cultural reception. By 2017, Dick's influence had spread far beyond American science fiction circles, propelled by film adaptations (Blade Runner 2049 was released in October 2017) and television series. A Glasgow newspaper covering Dick-related content reflects the global reach of his cultural legacy and the particular enthusiasm for his work in the United Kingdom, where he has long been taken more seriously as a literary figure than in his home country.
